summary:
Chemical interaction with dentin or enamel for adhesion purposes is a shared characteristic between these two materials due to their mechanisms of action at the microscopic level during application within restorative procedures. \boxed{B}

The primary focus here was establishing the basis for why an adhesive mechanism would be present across different classes of dental cements like those mentioned. Agent1 aimed to highlight fundamental similarities rather than specific differences which were not pertinent given just asking about one thing they have in common clinically speaking among options provided originally posed by Questioner.
